title: Cardano 2030: Vision, Mission, Strategy Framework and KPIs

rationale.summary: Vote: ABSTAIN. For this InfoAction (expired), evidence quality was insufficient for a reliable YES or NO decision. Primary blocker: evidence did not clear the directional decision threshold. Decisive reason: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "Starting at the end of 2024 and continuing throughout 2025, the Cardano Product Committee of Intersect facilitated the first community-led process to propose a 2030 Cardano Vision and Strategy ." and clears the evidence gates. What would enable a directional vote: Provide mitigation evidence; Provide independent assurance. Residual uncertainty: Rule-based system; does not infer unstated intent. Confidence context: score 0.11; confidence 62.7%.
rationale.summary_raw: DRep ratification support is above the hard-fork threshold; treated as a positive context signal.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Cardano 2030: Vision, Mission, Strategy Framework and KPIs
Stated amount: not stated in document

Deliverables: Starting at the end of 2024 and continuing throughout 2025, the Cardano Product Committee of Intersect facilitated the first community-led process to propose a 2030 Cardano Vision and Strategy ., During the first part of 2025 this process also received delegated authority and funding by Cardano DReps during the 2025 budget cycle., The extensive process the Product Committee followed, which involved 700+ people , focused on collecting as much input as possible from the broader community with attention to representative groups via different channels such as surveys, focus groups and re..., This process can be reused in the future to keep both Vision and Strategy updated (high level process mapped): 1.

Claim (governance, independently_verifiable, high materiality): Starting at the end of 2024 and continuing throughout 2025, the Cardano Product Committee of Intersect facilitated the first community-led process to propose a 2030 Cardano Vision and Strategy .
Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, high materiality): During the first part of 2025 this process also received delegated authority and funding by Cardano DReps during the 2025 budget cycle.

Claim (governance, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The extensive process the Product Committee followed, which involved 700+ people , focused on collecting as much input as possible from the broader community with attention to representative groups via different channels such as surveys, focus groups and re...
Claim (other, independently_verifiable, low materiality): This process can be reused in the future to keep both Vision and Strategy updated (high level process mapped): 1.

Claim (other, independently_verifiable, low materiality): Why a Defined Vision Matters Now More Than Ever In the rapidly evolving blockchain landscape, a clear and articulated vision is no longer a luxury, but a necessity.
Claim (other, independently_verifiable, low materiality): For Cardano, establishing a forward-looking Vision 2030 is crucial to ensure sustained growth, focused innovation, and broad legitimacy.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Starting at the end of 2024 and continuing throughout 2025, the Cardano Product Committee of Intersect facilitated the first community-led process to propose a 2030 Cardano Vision and Strategy ." and clears the evidence gates.
Strongest NO: weak controls, unclear delivery, or governance precedent could outweigh the benefit of InfoAction.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.

decision_contract.composite_formula: 40% ecosystem benefit + 25% delivery confidence + 20% cost efficiency + 15% downside protection - 0.50
decision_contract.benefit_formula: 18% critical infrastructure + 8% open source + 15% adoption + 12% ecosystem leverage + 10% strategic necessity + 10% prior delivery + 10% established service + 8% builder dependency + 5% low substitutability + 4% non-funding disruption risk
decision_contract.yes_rule: All quality floors, independently verified evidence, verified NCL, and portfolio capacity must pass.
decision_contract.no_rule: Requires affirmative independently verified waste/harm plus ecosystem benefit below 55%; missing evidence alone is never NO.
decision_contract.portfolio_gate: Verified on-chain Net Change Limit and opportunity-cost portfolio eligibility are mandatory for treasury direction.
decision_contract.execution_note: Merit is evaluated independently; the executable recommendation also requires verified NCL and portfolio capacity.
